news.title: Jaipur developer moves SC over alleged commercial use of residential land, seeks directions to civic authorities
news.para: The application has been filed in the pending matter of Loganathan vs State of Tamil Nadu & Ors., in which the Supreme Court, in March this year, directed municipal bodies across all state capitals to identify residential areas allegedly being used for commercial purposes and submit compliance reports.

news.title: Develop 11 mega industrial parks, create 50,000-acre land bank: Bihar CM
news.para: Bihar's Chief Minister Samrat Choudhary has directed the development of 11 mega industrial parks and food parks in all 38 districts to boost industrial growth and employment. The state aims to create a land bank of 50,000 acres and simplify procedures to become an attractive investment destination, focusing on sectors like food processing and textiles.

news.title: Maharashtra government revises base rates under sand extraction policy
news.para: Maharashtra government has revised sand extraction policy, introducing new base rates and royalty for eco-friendly sand derived from mining by-products. This initiative aims to reduce pressure on riverbeds and encourage industries producing sand from overburden, while also curbing illegal excavation and supporting the construction sector.

news.title: UP-RERA says over 1.15 lakh units available across Uttar Pradesh
news.para: UP-RERA has advised homebuyers and investors to make property purchase decisions after verifying project details and assessing their financial capacity. The authority said more than 1.15 lakh residential and commercial units are currently available across Uttar Pradesh, and buyers should not act under pressure created by claims of stock shortage or imminent price hikes.

news.title: NGT sets up panel to probe ‘illegal sand mining in Rushikulya river’
news.para: The National Green Tribunal has ordered a joint investigation into alleged illegal sand mining and environmental violations at a Rushikulya river sand quarry. Notices have been issued to state authorities and regulatory agencies following a petition highlighting irregularities, including mechanized extraction and exceeding lease areas. A committee will examine the site and recommend measures within a month.

news.title: Uttar Pradesh government to draft GIS-based master plans for 93 tier-II towns across 49 districts
news.para: Uttar Pradesh is preparing GIS-based master plans for 93 tier II towns with populations between 50,000 and 1 lakh to ensure planned development. This initiative aims to detail land usage and future projects for government agencies and stakeholders, addressing a gap in regulatory oversight for many of these urban local bodies.

news.title: Property tax defaulters in Chandigarh to soon get recovery notices
news.para: Chandigarh civic body is preparing to send recovery notices to approximately 70,000-75,000 property tax defaulters. These notices will include penalties and interest on unpaid taxes. Residential property defaulters are identified as the majority. Stern actions, including property sealing for commercial defaulters and water supply disconnection for residential defaulters, will be taken if taxes remain unpaid.

news.title: MCG faces property ID backlog after reshuffle of clerks in Gurugram, 77% files stuck
news.para: Gurugram's property tax branch is struggling with a large backlog of property objections and new ID applications. This follows a recent reshuffle of clerks amid corruption allegations. Many applications are stuck at the MCG level, delaying essential property transactions. Officials cite the transfers and census duties for the delay. They promise daily follow-ups to resolve the pending issues soon.

news.title: Over 5,000 applications to be clear under Majhe Ghar scheme: Goa CM
news.para: Goa Chief Minister Pramod Sawant announced over 5,000 Majhe Ghar scheme applications approved, with 3,000 orders ready for handover. He emphasized the government's commitment to preventing illegal construction, particularly by migrants, and streamlining processes for house regularisation on government and comunidade land.

news.title: Telangana government to revise property registration rates from June 5
news.para: Telangana is set to implement revised property registration values from June 5, the first major update in nearly a year. The revisions, ranging from 25% to 100% increases, aim to align government-notified rates with actual market values, particularly in urban core areas. This move follows extensive surveys and aims to bridge a significant gap identified between official and market prices.

news.title: Himachal Pradesh land pooling policy to be in line with successful models adopted by other states
news.para: Himachal Pradesh is set to implement a Land Pooling Policy, drawing inspiration from successful models in states like Punjab. This initiative aims to alleviate congestion in Shimla and other key areas by exploring new township developments and relocating wholesale markets. The government is also focusing on improving traffic flow in Shimla by addressing major bottlenecks and enhancing parking facilities.

news.title: Delhi to withdraw B&B policy after Malviya Nagar fire tragedy: Tourism minister
news.para: Following a devastating fire that killed 21 people, the Delhi government is withdrawing its Bed and Breakfast policy and reviewing all licensed establishments. The Flourish Stay B&B, allegedly operating with 25 rooms despite a six-room license and lacking fire safety clearance, is at the center of the probe. This incident highlights systemic issues in regulatory oversight and enforcement.

news.title: Uttar Pradesh cabinet approves funds for new city development in Agra, Bareilly, Prayagraj
news.para: Uttar Pradesh Cabinet approved funds for new city development in Agra, Bareilly, and Prayagraj. The Chief Minister Urban Expansion/New City Promotion Scheme will receive Rs 3,500 crore for the financial year 2026-27. Seed capital of Rs 355.06 crore is approved for these projects.

news.title: Delhi: MCD to seal unauthorised commercial buildings in South Zone after Malviya Nagar blaze
news.para: Following a fatal fire that killed 21 people, the Municipal Corporation of Delhi will begin sealing unauthorized commercial buildings in the South Zone. The bed-and-breakfast hotel, located in a Lal Dora village area, operated with 25 rooms despite permission for only six and lacked a sanctioned building plan.

news.title: Delhi home minister says buildings lacking fire safety NOC, other approvals to be sealed
news.para: Following a fatal fire in Malviya Nagar, Delhi Home Minister Ashish Sood announced that all buildings lacking required fire safety and other permissions will be sealed. An immediate city-wide enforcement drive and a crackdown on illegal hotels have been ordered. The tragedy highlighted serious violations at a B&B establishment, prompting a comprehensive inspection of similar facilities.

news.title: Insomniacs and YoUnique Communication launches YouBeYou
news.para: Insomniacs and YoUnique Communication have launched YouBeYou, a new integrated communications ecosystem for India's real estate and infrastructure brands. This venture combines Insomniacs' digital marketing and tech expertise with YoUnique's PR and brand storytelling strengths. YouBeYou aims to offer a unified approach to enhance brand visibility, credibility, and long-term reputation for the sector.

news.title: Kotak Alts closes fourteenth real estate fund at nearly $1 billion
news.para: Kotak Alternate Asset Managers has closed its fourteenth real estate fund at nearly $1 billion, backed by commitments from ADIA and NPS Korea. ADIA has made an anchor commitment of over $675 million, while NPS Korea has made its first investment in Indian alternative assets through the fund.

news.title: Propsoch raises $2 million in seed funding
news.para: Bengaluru-based Propsoch has secured USD 2 million in seed funding, with Athera Venture Partners, Sparrow Capital, and Vakil Group leading the round. This capital infusion will bolster research and advisory services, team expansion, and market growth. The company previously raised USD 600,000 in pre-seed funding.

news.title: SC sets up expert panel on Aravalis, petitioners question its independence
news.para: The Supreme Court has formed a five-member high-powered committee to redefine the 'Aravali hills and ranges', dropping the controversial 100m height criterion. This committee will assess the ecological impact of regulated mining and identify areas excluded from protection, aiming to prevent further degradation of the ancient mountain ranges.
